title: CIL Geology — Full-length Mock 10
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The pair of (A) / shoes are (B) / kept in (C) / the cupboard. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'is'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

What has improved mine safety according to the passage?
options: Real-time monitoring systems and gas detectors
questions.solution: Real-time monitoring systems and gas detectors

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: I have (A) / been working (B) / in this organisation (C) / since ten years. (D)
questions.solution: D - should be 'for ten years'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's installed nuclear power capacity is:
questions.solution: 7.5 GW

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: Hardly had (A) / he arrived (B) / than (C) / the meeting started. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'when'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Washeries are used to:
questions.solution: Reduce ash content in coal

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The company (A) / is planning to (B) / set up (C) / a new industry. (D)
questions.solution: No error

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

Continuous miners and longwall mining methods have increased:
questions.solution: Productivity and safety

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The data (A) / collected from (B) / the site is (C) / being analysed. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'are'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Gondwana coal fields are located in which part of India?
questions.solution: Eastern India

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: She was (A) / angry on (B) / me for (C) / being late. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'with'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: India's energy demand is projected to grow at 3-4% annually over the next decade. While coal remains the dominant source, accounting for about 55% of installed capacity, renewable energy is expanding rapidly. Solar power capacity has grown from just 2.6 GW in 2014 to over 70 GW in 2024. Wind power contributes another 45 GW. The government's Production-Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for solar manufacturing aims to reduce dependence on imports. Nuclear power, with 7.5 GW capacity, and hydropower, with 47 GW, are other important components of India's energy portfolio.

India's solar power capacity in 2024 is approximately:
questions.solution: 70 GW

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The price of (A) / these goods (B) / have increased (C) / significantly. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'has increased'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: The Gondwana coal fields of eastern India contain the country's best quality coal. These fields stretch across Jharkhand, West Bengal, Odisha, and Chhattisgarh. The coal found here is primarily bituminous, which has high calorific value and low ash content. In contrast, the Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain lignite and sub-bituminous coal. Coal grading is based on ash content, with higher grades having lower ash percentages. Washeries are used to reduce ash content and improve coal quality before it is supplied to power plants and industries.

Tertiary coal fields of northeastern India contain:
questions.solution: Lignite and sub-bituminous coal

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He asked (A) / me to (B) / convey him (C) / my regards. (D)
questions.solution: C - should be 'convey to him'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem:
Passage: Underground mining is one of the most hazardous occupations in the world. The primary risks include roof falls, gas explosions, flooding, and ventilation failures. In India, the Mines Act of 1952 and the Coal Mines Regulations of 2017 govern safety standards. Modern technology has significantly improved mine safety through real-time monitoring systems, gas detectors, and automated equipment. Despite these advances, safety remains a critical concern, and regular training programs are mandatory for all mine workers. The introduction of continuous miners and longwall mining methods has increased both productivity and safety.

The word 'mandatory' in the passage most nearly means:
questions.solution: Compulsory

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The number of (A) / accidents have (B) / increased this (C) / year compared to last. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'has'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: is / of / coal / formation / a / process / slow
options: Coal formation is a slow process
A slow process is formation of coal
Formation of coal is a slow process
Coal is a slow process of formation
questions.solution: Coal formation is a slow process

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The committee have submitted its report.
options: The committee have submitted its report.
The committee has submitted their report.
The committee has submitted its report.
The committee have submitted their report.
questions.solution: The committee has submitted its report.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: She prefers (A) / coffee than (B) / tea in (C) / the morning. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'to'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of BELLIGERENT:
questions.solution: Hostile

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He is (A) / suffering with (B) / high fever (C) / since last week. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'from'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of EPHEMERAL:
questions.solution: Transient

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The book (A) / which I (B) / was reading (C) / is interesting. (D)
questions.solution: No error

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ADMONISH:
questions.solution: Praise

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Depositional environment — tidal flat:
options: Supratidal (marsh), intertidal (mudflat, sandflat), subtidal (channel). Herringbone cross-bedding, flaser bedding, lenticular bedding
questions.solution: Tidal: bipolar cross-bedding (ebb + flood). Mud drapes. Reactivation surfaces.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Pencil cleavage forms when:
options: Two cleavages intersect at high angle
questions.solution: Pencil cleavage: intersection of two cleavages at ~90°. Rock breaks into pencil-shaped fragments. Common in fold hinges.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Core drilling:
options: Obtains intact rock core. Diamond bit. Core barrel. HQ, NQ, BQ sizes
questions.solution: Core sizes: HQ (63.5mm), NQ (47.6mm), BQ (36.5mm). Recovery ratio: % core recovered.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stretching lineation:
options: Long axis of deformed objects/grains. Parallel to X-strain axis. In L-tectonites (rodding)
questions.solution: L-tectonite: rod-like fabric. L-S tectonite: planar + linear. S-tectonite: planar only.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Landform types:
options: Fluvial (river), Glacial, Aeolian (wind), Coastal, Karst, Volcanic
questions.solution: Geomorphology: study of landforms and processes. Dynamic equilibrium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Komatiite flow differentiation:
options: Spinifex (olivine blades) + B-zone (random olivine). Thermal erosion channels. ~1600°C eruption T
questions.solution: Komatiite: Archean. High MgO (>18%). Channelized flow. Ni-sulfide deposits (Kambalda).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquifer parameter estimation: type curve matching:
options: Match observed drawdown curve to Theis type curve. Read T and S from match point
questions.solution: Theis type curve: W(u) vs 1/u. Match point gives T and S. Automated now (parameter estimation).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Miller indices (hkl):
options: Intercepts on crystal axes. (111): octahedron. (100): cube. (110): dodecahedron. Negative: bar over index
questions.solution: Miller: reciprocal of intercepts. Zero = parallel to axis. Dhkl = a/√(h²+k²+l²) (cubic).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shear zone vs fault: shear zones are ductile deformation at depth, faults are brittle at shallow levels. Transition occurs at:
questions.solution: BDT: quartz: ~300°C, feldspar: ~450°C. Depth depends on geothermal gradient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The boundary separating the crust from the mantle is the:
questions.solution: The Moho marks the crust–mantle boundary, identified by a jump in seismic velocity.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Crystal system with 6-fold symmetry axis is:
questions.solution: Hexagonal: one 6-fold axis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Oxygen isotopes — δ¹⁸O:
options: Paleoclimate proxy. Marine δ¹⁸O → ice volume + T. Foraminifera tests. G. bulloides, G. ruber
questions.solution: δ¹⁸O: warmer = lighter (more ¹⁶O incorporated). Marine isotope stages (MIS). VSMOW, PDB standards.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Landfill leachate contains:
questions.solution: Leachate: water percolating through waste picks up contaminants. Requires liner and collection system. Contaminates groundwater.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a station the S-wave arrives 15 s after the P-wave. Using ~8 km per second of S–P delay, the epicentral distance is about:
questions.solution: distance ≈ (S−P time) × 8 km/s = 15×8 = 120 km.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Bioturbation index measures:
questions.solution: BI: 0-6 scale. 0 = no bioturbation, 6 = complete homogenization. Reflects oxygenation, sedimentation rate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porosity types in carbonates:
options: Interparticle, intraparticle, moldic, vuggy, fenestral, fracture, channel. Secondary porosity dominant
questions.solution: Carbonates: primary porosity low after diagenesis. Secondary: dissolution (moldic, vuggy). Choquette-Pray classification.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Slickenside striations indicate:
questions.solution: Slickensides: polished/striated fault surface. Striations parallel to movement direction. Riedel shears indicate sense.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: TAS diagram (volcanic):
options: Total Alkali vs Silica. Le Maitre et al. Classification of volcanic rocks
questions.solution: TAS: 15 fields. Basalt, andesite, dacite, rhyolite, trachyte, phonolite. Alkaline vs subalkaline.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Partial melting reactions in pelite:
options: Muscovite + quartz + plagioclase + H₂O → melt (migmatite). Biotite dehydration melting at higher T
questions.solution: Pelite: fluid-present (Ms breakdown) → fluid-absent (Bt dehydration). P-T of solidus varies.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Viewshed analysis:
options: Area visible from point(s). Line-of-sight. For towers, observation points
questions.solution: Viewshed: cumulative visibility. Observer height + target height. Multiple observers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Facies model — braided river:
options: Sheet-like, multiple channels. Bar forms (longitudinal, transverse, diagonal). Vertically stacked, pebbly
questions.solution: Braided: flashy discharge. Gravel sheets. No fine-grained overbank. Non-cyclic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral specimen of mass 78 g has a volume of 30 cm³. Its specific gravity is about:
questions.solution: SG = mass/volume = 78/30 = 2.6.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sandstone is a:
options: Clastic sedimentary rock made of sand-sized grains
questions.solution: Sandstone is a clastic rock of cemented sand (mostly quartz) grains.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Seismic reflection survey: Common Midpoint (CMP) gathering improves:
questions.solution: CMP: multiple traces share same midpoint. Stack after NMO correction. Improves S/N by sqrt(n) where n = fold.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Image classification: supervised vs unsupervised:
options: Supervised: training sites (known classes). Unsupervised: clustering (unknown classes)
questions.solution: Supervised: ML, Maximum Likelihood, SVM. Unsupervised: K-means, ISODATA.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about coalification is correct?
options: It increases fixed carbon and reduces volatile matter
questions.solution: Progressive burial heat raises rank: more fixed carbon, less moisture and volatiles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ophiolite emplacement: how does oceanic crust get on top of continental?
questions.solution: Obduction: oceanic crust thrust onto continent. Oman ophiolite. SSZ (supra-subduction zone) type.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ore microscopy: reflected light identification:
options: Pyrite: high reflectance, yellow, hard. Chalcopyrite: brass yellow, soft. Galena: bright white, cubic
questions.solution: Ore microscopy: polished section. Reflectance, color, hardness (streak), bireflectance, anisotropy.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Archean cratons in India:
options: Dharwar (W & E), Singhbhum, Bastar, Bundelkhand, Rajasthan (Aravalli)
questions.solution: Dharwar Craton: 3.4-2.5 Ga. Divided by Closepet Granite into E and W Dharwar.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Volcanic landforms:
options: Shield volcano (basalt), stratovolcano (andesite), cinder cone, caldera, lava dome
questions.solution: Shield: Hawaii (gentle slopes). Stratovolcano: Fuji, Vesuvius (steep, explosive). Caldera: Crater Lake.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compared with surface (opencast) mining, underground coal mining is generally chosen when the seam is:
options: Deep, so that the stripping ratio becomes uneconomic
questions.solution: When overburden-to-coal (stripping) ratio is too high for opencast, underground methods become economic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which pairing of fault type and stress is correct?
questions.solution: Tension produces normal faults; compression produces reverse/thrust faults.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transition zone — 410 km & 660 km:
options: Olivine → wadsleyite (410 km) and ringwoodite → perovskite + magnesiowüstite (660 km). Phase changes
questions.solution: Transition zone: seismic velocity jumps. Topography on 410 and 660 = mantle T anomalies.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stromatolites: layered sedimentary structures formed by:
questions.solution: Stromatolite: laminated, domical. Cyanobacteria trap/bind sediment. Shark Bay (Australia).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ammonite biostratigraphy:
options: Mesozoic index fossils. Rapid evolution, wide distribution. Ceratitic vs ammonitic suture lines
questions.solution: Ammonites: Mollusca, Cephalopoda. Suture: goniatitic → ceratitic → ammonitic (increasing complexity).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Paleoenvironmental indicators: evaporites indicate:
options: Arid, restricted basin. High evaporation, limited inflow
questions.solution: Evaporite: sabkha, restricted lagoon, saline lake. Arid climate indicator.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Radiolaria: siliceous microfossils. Used for:
questions.solution: Radiolaria: SiO2 tests. Deep sea sediments. Biostratigraphic zonation for Mesozoic-Cenozoic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Angular unconformity:
options: Older folded strata overlain by horizontal strata
questions.solution: Angular: represents orogeny + erosion + renewed deposition. Great Unconformity (Grand Canyon).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 40 m of overburden above a 3 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 40/3 = 13.33 : 1.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 140" width="260" height="140"><rect x="20" y="20" width="220" height="60" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="54" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#92400e">overburden 40 m</text><rect x="20" y="80" width="220" height="20" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="130" y="94" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#fff">coal seam 3 m</text><rect x="20" y="100" width="220" height="30" fill="#d1d5db" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1"/><text x="130" y="120" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#374151">floor</text></svg>
figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The upper surface of the zone of saturation in the ground is the:
questions.solution: The water table marks the top of the saturated zone, below which pores are water-filled.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quartz belongs to:
questions.solution: Quartz: SiO2 framework. No cleavage. Conchoidal fracture. Piezoelectric.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Esker:
options: Ridge of stratified sand/gravel deposited by subglacial stream
questions.solution: Esker: sinuous ridge. Glaciofluvial. Useful aggregate resource.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GPS (Global Positioning System): minimum 4 satellites for:
questions.solution: 4 satellites: solve for 4 unknowns (x,y,z,dt). More satellites improve geometry (DOP).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reservoir sedimentation:
options: Sediment deposition reduces storage capacity. Dead storage + live storage
questions.solution: Sedimentation: rate depends on catchment erosion. Flushing, sluicing. Check dams in tributaries.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vesicular texture:
questions.solution: Vesicles: gas bubbles trapped in lava. Amygdules: filled vesicles.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biostratigraphic zone: range zone defined by:
options: First and last appearance of index fossil
questions.solution: Range zone: FAD to LAD of index fossil. Assemblage zone: characteristic assemblage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hummocky cross-stratification (HCS):
options: Formed by storm waves below fair-weather wave base. Undulating sets, low angle
questions.solution: HCS: diagnostic of storm-dominated shelves. Hummocks: 3D cross-sets. Swales: concave down.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: P-T path (clockwise):
options: Burial→heating→exhumation. Typical for collisional orogens. Andalusite→kyanite→sillimanite
questions.solution: Clockwise: Barrovian. ITD (isothermal decompression). Counterclockwise: high T/low P (Buchan).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Well logging — gamma ray:
options: Natural γ (K, U, Th). Shale indicator (high API). Clean sand: low GR. Spectral GR: Th/U ratio for redox
questions.solution: GR: permeable zones (low GR). Shale volume: V_sh = (GR - GR_clean)/(GR_shale - GR_clean).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 2 Mt has an average metal grade of 3%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 2 Mt × 3% = 60 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Paleomagnetic dating: magnetic polarity reversals used for dating because:
options: Reversals are global, synchronous events recorded in rocks
questions.solution: GPTS: Global Polarity Time Scale. Reversals: worldwide. Correlation of magnetozone to GPTS.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Competent vs incompetent layers in folding:
options: Competent layers buckle and are thicker at hinges. Incompetent flow into hinges
questions.solution: Competent: quartzite, sandstone. Incompetent: shale, salt. Parasitic folds in incompetent layers.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about coal ranks is INCORRECT?
options: Lignite has a higher carbon content than anthracite
questions.solution: Anthracite, not lignite, has the highest carbon content — the statement is reversed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 25 m/day) of cross-section 200 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fluorite (fluorite) hardness:
questions.solution: CaF2. Perfect octahedral cleavage. Fluorescence. Used in optics.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Denudation rate estimation: cosmogenic nuclides (¹⁰Be, ²⁶Al):
options: Accumulation in surface rocks. Higher concentration = slower erosion
questions.solution: ¹⁰Be: production by cosmic rays. Concentration inversely related to denudation rate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Least cost path analysis:
options: Path minimizing cumulative cost (distance × weight). For pipelines, roads, transmission lines
questions.solution: Cost surface: slope, land use, geology, environmental constraints. Dijkstra's algorithm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rock bolt types:
options: Mechanical (expansion shell), Resin-grouted (rebar), Friction (Split set, Swellex)
questions.solution: Rock bolts: tensioned or untensioned. Ground support in tunnels. Pattern or spot bolting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Igneous rock classification by silica content:
questions.solution: Granite (acid), Andesite (intermediate), Basalt (basic), Peridotite (ultrabasic).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Deccan Traps:
questions.solution: Deccan: K-Pg boundary. Reunion hotspot. Intertrappean sedimentary beds.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentary rock formation stages:
options: Weathering → erosion → transport → deposition → compaction → cementation
questions.solution: Lithification: compaction + cementation. Cement: calcite, silica, iron oxide.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydrothermal metamorphism:
questions.solution: Hydrothermal: mid-ocean ridges. Greenschist facies alteration of basalt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An ore body of 2 Mt has an average metal grade of 2%. The contained metal is about:
questions.solution: Metal = tonnage × grade = 2 Mt × 2% = 40 thousand tonnes (kt).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 5 m³ of coal, 45 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 45/5 = 9 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most abundant single mineral group in the crust is:
questions.solution: Feldspars make up roughly half of the Earth's crust by volume.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Image processing — atmospheric correction:
options: Remove scattering and absorption effects. Dark object subtraction (DOS), FLAASH, 6S
questions.solution: Atmospheric: Rayleigh + Mie scattering. Water vapor absorption. DOS: assumes dark pixel = 0 reflectance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Deccan Traps intertrappean beds:
options: Sedimentary layers between basalt flows. Contain fossils (dinosaur eggs, plants)
questions.solution: Intertrappean: limestone, sandstone, shale. Rajamundry, Jabalpur. Dinosaur eggs at Lameta.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic tomography: 3D velocity model from:
options: Travel time residuals of many ray paths through Earth
questions.solution: Tomography: inverse of many travel times. High velocity = cold/dense. Low velocity = hot/fluid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Thermogravimetric Analysis (TGA) measures:
questions.solution: TGA: mass loss = dehydration, decomposition. Steps in curve = distinct reactions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magmatic differentiation:
options: Processes that produce compositionally diverse rocks from single parent magma
questions.solution: Differentiation: fractional crystallization, liquid immiscibility, crystal settling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Specific yield S_y:
options: Volume drained by gravity / total volume. Unconfined aquifer storage
questions.solution: S_y: sand 0.2-0.3, clay 0.02-0.05. S_y + S_r = porosity (S_r = specific retention).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granite composition:
questions.solution: Granite: coarse-grained, felsic. Common in continental crust.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 10 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 30% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+30+10) = 56%.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Ghyben-Herzberg relation describes:
questions.solution: z = 40h. Freshwater head h corresponds to z depth of interface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Denudation chronology: Davis cycle:
options: Youth (high relief, rapid incision) → Maturity (maximum dissection) → Old age (peneplain, low relief)
questions.solution: Davis: time-dependent. Criticized for oversimplifying. Penck's alternative emphasizes uplift rate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: XRD Bragg's law: nλ = 2d·sin(θ). For Cu Kα (λ=1.54Å), d=3Å, n=1:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reverse/thrust fault:
options: Hanging wall moves up (compression). Thrust: <45° dip
questions.solution: Reverse: shortening. Thrust: low angle (<45°). Nappes: large displacement thrusts.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Olivine belongs to:
questions.solution: Olivine (Mg,Fe)2SiO4 — high-T mineral. Mantle rock peridotite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pumping test — recovery:
options: Residual drawdown s' = (2.303Q)/(4πT)·log(t/t'). No S estimate needed
questions.solution: Recovery: T from slope of s' vs log(t/t'). More reliable than drawdown (constant rate).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Liptinite (exinite) macerals origin:
options: Waxy, resinous plant parts (spores, cuticles, algae, resins)
questions.solution: Liptinite: sporinite, cutinite, alginite, resinite. High hydrogen, oil-prone.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fold axis determination:
options: Beta (β) axis: intersection of two limbs. Pi (π) girdle: poles to bedding
questions.solution: π-diagram: poles to bedding on girdle. β-axis: maximum density point on π-circle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Refractive index of minerals:
options: Determined by Becke line method. Mineral vs mounting medium
questions.solution: Becke line: bright line moves into higher RI medium when stage is lowered.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Capillary fringe:
options: Zone above water table where water is held by capillary forces. Saturation > 0
questions.solution: Capillary rise: fine sand 1-2m, silt 2-5m, clay >10m. Negative pressure.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Darcy velocity is:
options: Volumetric flow rate per unit cross-sectional area
questions.solution: q = Q/A = Ki. Apparent velocity through total area.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Closepet Granite:
options: Granite batholith between E and W Dharwar Cratons. ~2.5 Ga. Late Archean
questions.solution: Closepet: N-S trending. High-K, calc-alkaline. Anatectic origin. Mantle+crustal source.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Phosphorite deposits:
options: Francolite (carbonate fluorapatite). Upwelling zones. Peru-Chile, Morocco
questions.solution: Phosphorite: P₂O₅ > 18%. Important fertilizer source. Pelletal or nodular.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal twinning: contact twin vs penetration twin:
options: Contact: shared plane (mirror). Penetration: shared point (interpenetrating)
questions.solution: Contact: staurolite, gypsum. Penetration: fluorite, pyrite, garnet twins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Debris flow mitigation:
options: Check dams, deflection walls, sabo dams, early warning
questions.solution: Debris flow: high velocity mixture of soil, rock, water. Runout distance analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Symplectite:
options: Intergrowth of two minerals (e.g., Cpx + Pl) replacing higher-P assemblage. Opx + Cpx + Pl from Grt breakdown
questions.solution: Symplectite: vermicular texture. Decompression reaction. Fingerprint of rapid exhumation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Earthquake magnitude vs intensity:
questions.solution: Moment magnitude (Mw): most accurate. MMI: I (not felt) to XII (total destruction).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granite classification — S, I, A, M types:
options: S (sedimentary source, peraluminous), I (igneous source, metaluminous), A (anorogenic, alkaline), M (mantle)
questions.solution: S-type: muscovite, monazite. I-type: hornblende, titanite. A-type: fluorite, high Ga/Al.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 35% volatile matter and 20% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+35+20) = 41%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 500 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m seam over 4 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.85. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 4M m²×3×1.4×0.85 ≈ 14.3 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock sample of 100 cm³ contains 15 cm³ of pore space. Its porosity is:
questions.solution: Porosity = (void volume/total volume)×100 = 15/100 = 15%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Porosity in fault zones:
options: Core: low porosity (gouge). Damage zone: high fracture porosity. Conduit-barrier
questions.solution: Fault seal: clay smear, cataclasis. Juxtaposition seal. Membrane seal (capillary entry pressure).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Texture of komatiite:
questions.solution: Komatiite: Archean ultramafic volcanic. Spinifex due to rapid cooling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 25% of its original parent isotope (half-life 4.47 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 25% remaining ⇒ 2 half-lives ⇒ age = 2×4.47 = 8.94 Gyr.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Microwave remote sensing — radar:
options: Active sensor. Day/night, cloud penetration. SAR: synthetic aperture
questions.solution: Radar: transmits pulses. Backscatter depends on roughness, moisture, geometry. Interferometry.
